rulings.Case Title: PHILIP H. HERNANDEZ VS. CITY & COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For A Writ Of Administrative Mandamus
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for August 20, 2019 line 1. PETITIONER PHILIP HERNANDEZ Motion For A Writ Of Administrative Mandamus 1. "Only those occupants who reside in the apartment at the start of the tenancy and do so with the landlord's express or implicit consent are protected from unregulated rent increases. Family members and friends who subsequently move into the apartment are not protected unless the landlord consents to the occupancy and accepts rent from the new occupant, thus creating a new tenancy." Mosser Companies v. San Francisco Rent Stabilization & Arbitration Bd. (2015) 233 Cal.App.4th 505, 516. Is this an exclusive way to create a new tenancy for purposes of Costa-Hawkins protections (consent and acceptance of rent) or there are other ways to create a new tenancy for Costa-Hawkins purposes (e.g. consent alone; consent and something else, etc.). If any party claims that this method of creating a new tenancy for the purposes of Costa-Hawkins protection is not exclusive, any new case authority is to be provided to the other side and the Court prior to the hearing. 2. Did the ALJ find a new tenancy where Acu and Rodriguez were "original occupants" was created in or about 2011? If so, is that tenancy protected under Costa-Hawkins per Mosser Companies v. San Francisco Rent Stabilization & Arbitration Bd. (2015) 233 Cal.App.4th 505. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: CHARLES GREENBERG ET AL VS. SVC-WEST, L.L.C. ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for August 20, 2019 line 2. Demurrer To Complaint SUSTAINED with leave to amend for Plaintiffs to allege facts (not conclusions) given the contents of the contracts showing reasonable reliance; facts giving rise to duty to disclose; allege an actual present controversy; and allege facts in support of each element of the cause of action for elder abuse. = (501/REQ)
